items.title: Canada just lowered a 1,050-ton slab of steel and concrete into a 115-foot shaft in Ontario and ended a decade of talk, starting the Western world's first grid-scale small nuclear reactor, a machine that fits on two soccer fields and powers 300,000 homes
items.blurb: The small modular reactor has been the nuclear industry's favorite slide for about a decade. Utilities pitch it, governments model it, conference panels argue
